preamble: The phonetic transformation from the close-mid back rounded vowel /o/ to the diphthong /au/ involves a process of diphthongization, where the monophthongal vowel develops into a gliding vowel. This shift typically entails a lowering of the initial vowel component and the addition of a back rounded glide.
phoneticEffects: Diphthongization, lowering of the initial vowel component, backing and rounding of the final vowel component

examples.note: Proto-Tibeto-Burman *do diphthongized to Burmese tau, which later became Modern -a.
Proto-Tibeto-Burman *pryo diphthongized to Burmese prau, which later became Modern -a.

sources: Benedict, P. K. (1972). Sino-Tibetan: A Conspectus. Cambridge University Press.
